stories.headline: Zelenskyy says Nato should let Ukraine join to ‘make all of us stronger’
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president says defensive capabilities built up in war with Russia mean it would be wrong to exclude itEurope live – latest updatesVolodymyr Zelenskyy has argued for Ukraine to be allowed to join Nato at its annual summit – saying it would be wrong to exclude a country that had built up strong defences in its long struggle against the Russian invasion.The Ukrainian president said his country had developed almost all the weapons it needed

stories.headline: Trump renews call for US to take over Greenland as he arrives for Nato summit
stories.summary: President also threatens to pull all American troops from Europe and rails against Nato stance on Iran warEurope live – latest updatesDonald Trump has revived his bid for the US to acquire Greenland, threatening to pull all American armed forces out of Europe after it repeatedly pushed back.Arriving at the Nato summit in Ankara on Tuesday, the US president also suggested his commitment to defending Europe had been tempered by political decisions

stories.headline: Le Pen to give TV interview as French presidential bid in doubt after court ruling - Europe live
stories.summary: Appeal court shortened far-right leader’s ban ⁠on running for elected office but ordered that she wear an ankle tagThe opening speeches are now under way in Ankara, and you can watch them below.This is the Day 1 industry event, not the leaders’ summit, mind you. stories.headline: Zelenskyy renews call for Ukraine's NATO membership citing military might ahead of summit
stories.summary: The Ukrainian President has renewed his push to join NATO, insisting his country has already proved itself a reliable partner and that its membership in the alliance would be “entirely natural”.

stories.headline: China test-fires long-range missile into Pacific, rattling U.S. allies
stories.summary: The unprecedented test of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile highlights Beijing’s growing force in the region.

stories.headline: Nigel Farage says he's resigning as MP and will run in 'people vs. the establishment' by-election
stories.summary: "I've decided that the people of Clacton should be the judges of my actions," Farage said. "It's a chance to stick two fingers up to the entire establishment".

stories.headline: Marine Le Pen found guilty, but court clears way for presidential run if she wears tag
stories.summary: The National Rally leader now has to decide whether she will run with an electronic tag and will speak on TV later.

stories.headline: Two mothers, each certain a viral Gaza prisoner photo shows her son
stories.summary: Two Palestinian mothers believe a viral image of a bound Gaza detainee shows their missing son.

stories.headline: French far-right leader Le Pen cleared to run for president
stories.summary: Appeals court shortens sentence for embezzlement but she must now decide whether to run

stories.headline: Nigel Farage quits as MP to force by-election
stories.summary: Tories and Labour refuse to participate in contest as Reform UK leader denies wrongdoing over donations

stories.headline: Midtown Manhattan Buildings Evacuated After Collapse Warning
stories.summary: Construction crews were pulled from a 37-story Midtown Manhattan building Tuesday morning after bricks were reported falling from the 21st floor, emergency officials said, prompting building evacuations and street closures. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: France appeals court opens door for Le Pen presidential run, with ankle tag
stories.summary: Paris appeals court rules Le Pen guilty of misusing public funds but reduces the ban on her holding public office.

stories.headline: Nigel Farage resigns as MP, will run in UK by-election
stories.summary: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage says he will resign as an MP to trigger a by-election and stand again.

stories.headline: Reform UK’s Farage resigns as MP amid funding scandal, forcing by-election
stories.summary: Nigel Farage says he's 'done nothing wrong' as parliament investigates undeclared benefits he accepted from a fraudster.

stories.headline: China missile test over Pacific alarms island nations
Fatal Typhoon Maysak floods burst dam wall in China

stories.headline: MIdtown Manhattan Buildings Evacuated After Officials Warn of Collapse

stories.headline: Abandoned by Allies, Graham Platner Faces Pressure to End Maine Senate Campaign
stories.summary: After Graham Platner, the Democratic nominee in Maine, was accused of rape, much of the party and several key supporters turned against him.

stories.headline: 6 potential replacements for Graham Platner if he drops out of Senate race
stories.summary: The Maine Democrat has faced an escalating string of scandals as he competes for a critical Senate seat that could determine control of the chamber.

stories.headline: Top Democrats pull Graham Platner endorsements, call on him to end Maine Senate campaign
stories.summary: The Democrat, who has withstood other scandals since launching his challenge to Sen. Susan Collins (R), is accused of sexual assault by a woman he previously dated.

stories.headline: Tokyo's property boom is a warning, not a crisis

stories.summary: As reports of the blasts came in, state television said the Syrian president had welcomed Macron at the presidential palace.

stories.headline: China Buys More US Soybeans as Key Agricultural Trade Ramps Up
stories.summary: China has purchased more soybeans from the US, according to people familiar with the matter, as the key agricultural trade gains momentum following a summit between the leaders of the two nations in May.

stories.headline: Lawsuit Alleges Trump Administration Gave Iran Details on Asylum Seekers
stories.summary: A new lawsuit filed in the District of Columbia states that the administration allowed Iranian officials to “select” which Iranians seeking refuge in the United States would be expelled.

stories.headline: Trump’s Tenuous Relationship With NATO Allies
stories.summary: At this year’s NATO summit in Turkey, President Trump is arriving with his own priorities — and, as Tyler Pager, The Times’s White House correspondent, explains, they do not necessarily align with those of other NATO leaders.

stories.headline: Platner’s Democratic Support Evaporates After Sexual Assault Allegation
stories.summary: Graham Platner, the Democratic Senate candidate in Maine, called the allegation false but said he would “reflect” on his political path forward, as many allies rescinded their endorsements.

stories.headline: U.S. gave Tehran details on Iranian asylum seekers, lawsuit alleges
stories.summary: The claim, outlined in court papers, contends information shared with Iran could jeopardize the lives of pro-democracy protesters, religious minorities and LGTBQ people.

SpaceX stock dropped precipitously after its initial public offering, and Tesla faces a wave of lawsuits. stories.headline: Displaced Gaza farmers turn to dirt near tents to grow crops
stories.summary: Israel has devastated 95 percent of Gaza's farms, engineering starvation and aid dependency, experts warn.

stories.headline: Ships attacked in the Strait of Hormuz: What that means for ongoing talks
stories.summary: While marine traffic in Hormuz has steadily risen, vague provisions on the strait remain contentious in peace talks.

stories.headline: India's landmark Ram temple overhauls board after alleged theft of donations
stories.summary: Questions have been raised over alleged theft of cash, valuable jewellery, gold and silver offered by devotees.

stories.headline: Why some African nations are turning down Trump aid money
stories.summary: The Trump administration sees the aid as more transactional - but some say it is not a fair trade.

stories.headline: Chile’s New Pension Rules Spark Industry Divide on Effects
stories.summary: Analysts, strategists and investors are struggling to weigh the impact — if any — a new investment regime for Chile’s pension funds will have on the broader market.

stories.headline: Italy Sells $6 Billion in First Dollar-Bond Sale Since 2021
stories.summary: Italy returned to bond market for the first time since the pandemic, broadening its pool of foreign investors after the country’s improving finances won credit-rating upgrades.

stories.headline: Samsung’s Record Profit Fails to Impress After AI Chip Rally
stories.summary:
For years, the Magnificent Seven tech giants commanded investors’ attention, dominating the S&P 500 Index and determining which way the overall stock market was headed. Those days are over.
While the technology-heavy Nasdaq 100 Index is up 16% in 2026 and the S&P 500 has climbed 10%, an index of the Mag 7 has gained just 1.7%. 
Ed Ludlow, BTech Anchor, addresses the top tech stories, including the Mag 7 struggling to keep a hold on the ma

stories.headline: Getty’s Lenders Organize on Renewed Risk of ‘Unsustainable’ Debt
stories.summary: Lenders to Getty Images Holdings Inc. have started to organize after the debt-laden photo archive business scrapped a planned merger with Shutterstock Inc., according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: BB Energy Strikes Deal with South Sudan to Unblock Oil Sales
stories.summary: BB Energy agreed to relax an injunction on the government of South Sudan in exchange for receiving three cargoes of oil, in a significant easing of tensions in the $142 million legal dispute between the trading house and the African nation.

On this episode of Stock Movers:

- Fiserv (FISV) is climbing as the Wall Street Journal reports big banks including JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Wells Fargo and PNC Financial Services Group have held preliminary discussions about a deal to acquire a network owned by the financial-technology company Fiserv.

stories.headline: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda.Catnip, or Nepeta cataria, is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria – nepetalactone – also has insect-repelling properties but this

stories.headline: Austrian campaign aims to save writer Stefan Zweig’s Salzburg villa after Porsche tunnel row
stories.summary: Supporters hope to stop 17th-century Villa Europa passing into private hands after Wolfgang Porsche unexpectedly put it on marketAustrian cultural figures have launched a campaign to buy a villa once home to the writer Stefan Zweig after its owner, the automotive magnate Wolfgang Porsche, unexpectedly put it on the market following a row over his plans to build a private tunnel for his car collection.Zweig, the Austrian Jewish writer whose novels

stories.headline: US trade deficit surges amid artificial intelligence spending boom
stories.summary: Imbalance soars to $77.6bn in May as imports outpaced exports, driven by pharmaceuticals and semiconductors.

Pakistan gives foreign banks an edge in shift to Islamic finance
Indian security analysts alarmed by Bangladesh-China port deal
Morocco multiplies its olive oil sales in Spain by 100: is it taking over the market?
stories.summary: Imports of Moroccan olive oil have surged 9,979% in a year, according to DataComex. The figure is real but starts from just 103 tonnes and its share in Spain remains small.

stories.headline: EU still can't prove €43 billion in home renovation funds actually saved energy
stories.summary: The EU accelerated home renovations after the pandemic, but according to its own auditors, it still lacks convincing evidence that the program delivered the energy savings and value for taxpayers that justified its massive investment.

stories.headline: ECB tells Europe's biggest banks to prepare for AI-powered cyber threats
stories.summary: The European Central Bank has given the eurozone's largest lenders until the end of October to explain how they will strengthen their cyber defences against increasingly capable AI systems.

stories.headline: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 23 killed in Kyiv region
stories.summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

stories.headline: China tests missile in the Pacific hours after Australia-Fiji alliance signed
stories.summary: The test launch came hours after Australia signed a new defence pact with its Pacific island neighbour Fiji.

stories.headline: After America’s 250th, Trump will test how far he can push NATO allies
stories.summary: As NATO leaders gather in Turkey, they are preparing for another summit shaped as much by managing President Donald Trump as by the alliance's security agenda.

stories.headline: Who Might Replace Graham Platner if He Drops Out of the Maine Senate Race?
stories.summary: Graham Platner can be replaced on the ballot if he withdraws in the next week. If he does, Maine Democrats would face an uncertain two-week race to choose a replacement.

stories.headline: Typhoon Maysak kills two and forces thousands to evacuate in China
stories.summary: Tropical storm causes extreme flooding in south of the country with heavy rainfall expected in coming daysA tropical storm has killed two people, caused dam breaches and forced tens of thousands to evacuate in southern China.Typhoon Maysak killed two people in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi province. stories.headline: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
stories.summary: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.

stories.headline: Chip Stocks Slide on Worries Rally Has Run Too Far: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A selloff in chipmakers dragged down the stock market on concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations after a breakneck surge from war-driven lows.

stories.headline: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
stories.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised for remarks he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.

stories.headline: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
stories.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
stories.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament
stories.summary: Fifteen women are appointed, after only six female candidates were selected by regional electoral colleges last October.

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas
